element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns foundational inputs before tournament begins
Introduced gradually as player progresses through opponents
Easier to chain attacks compared to first game; rewards skill development
New addition to combat depth; low/high kick differentiation increases tactical play
High-damage moves that knock opponent across screen; require spacing awareness
Advanced technique requiring aerial awareness and timing; opponents use these strategically
Finishing move mechanic; player learns character-specific inputs after victory conditions
Optional finishing move providing tonal contrast to lethal alternatives
Character-specific positive interactions available as finisher alternative
Environmental finishing moves (acid pools, spike traps, abysses); stage-dependent availability
Supreme Outworld ruler with home field advantage; represents significant difficulty spike
Advanced discovery; available after meeting unlock criteria
Boss and secret characters cannot use finishing moves, creating asymmetrical mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Winning a round and entering finishing move sequence
Babality or Friendship finisher (tonal shift from violence)
typical_tension_triggers: Life bar depletion approaching critical state
Boss character encounters (Shao Kahn with home field advantage)
Mid-air special move sequences requiring reads
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced tournament structure with escalating opponent difficulty; gameplay plays almost twice as fast as original, creating continuous tension with relief cycles tied to match victories and finishing move sequences

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Stage-specific Fatalities available in specific environments; hidden character Noob Saibot unlocked through specific conditions
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden character discovery and stage-specific environmental finishers reward exploration of game mechanics and environment interaction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic combat system with foundational opponents; easier to execute combos due to reduced recovery times; establishes core mechanics before intensity escalates
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased opponent variety and AI difficulty; introduction of mid-air special moves and character-specific strategies; tournament opponents become progressively more skilled
difficulty_curve.late_game: Shao Kahn final boss fight with home field advantage; significantly higher difficulty spike; opponent possesses superior martial prowess and tactical positioning
notable_spikes: Transition to mid-tournament stages with opponents using mid-air special moves
Final tournament bracket approaching Shao Kahn
Shao Kahn encounter (supreme ruler with environmental advantage)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear tournament structure where player advances through successive opponents of increasing difficulty, with each victory enabling finishing move execution. Tournament relocated to Outworld (neutral ground advantage for Kahn) creates escalating stakes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tournament rounds 1-N leading to Shao Kahn

notable_level_patterns.description: Specific stages feature environmental finishing moves unique to that location (spike ceilings, acid pools, abyss drops), creating stage-specific tactical depth and reward for environmental awareness.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Pit II stage with stage-specific fatality mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple finisher categories (Fatalities, Babalities, Friendships, Stage Fatalities) provide tonal and mechanical variety after round victory, creating pacing relief and character expression opportunities.

sources_used: tcrf - Mortal Kombat II (SNES) technical documentation
wikipedia - Mortal Kombat II gameplay and mechanics description
